nativeRange.habitat: Temporary stagnant freshwater pools, ponds, and seasonally flooded wetlands of the Indian subcontinent; bulb survives dry-season dormancy and re-sprouts when rains return.

decorPreferences: bulb planted with top half exposed

summary: An easy Indian/Sri Lankan bulb plant whose long-stemmed lance-shaped leaves typically reach the surface and lie flat as floating leaves; popular as a low-effort background centerpiece.
careNotes: Aponogeton natans is a tropical bulb plant from temporary freshwater pools across India and Sri Lanka. The 'Floating Hearts' common name overlaps with several Nymphoides species (e.g., Nymphoides aquatica), so always confirm the scientific name when buying — true Aponogeton natans is a bulb-rooted plant with a single dark green lance-shaped leaf blade per stem, not the heart-shaped lily pads of Nymphoides. Plant the bulb with roughly the top half above the substrate to prevent rot; new shoots emerge from the exposed crown. At maturity it tends to send long-stemmed leaves to the surface where they spread as floating leaves — this is normal behaviour for the species. To maintain a fully submerged form, trim the longest leaves before they reach the surface; the plant will continue pushing new leaves from the bulb. Typical reported height is 20–50 cm, with vigorous specimens occasionally taller under strong light. Temperature tolerance is broad (sources span 20–28 °C, with Flowgrow noting up to ~31 °C); pH is tolerant from roughly 6.0–7.5; hardness 2–14 °dGH/dKH. Lighting is flexible — low-to-medium light is sufficient, and medium light produces the densest, healthiest growth. CO2 is optional and not required for good growth. Use as a background plant in medium-to-large tanks where the floating-leaf habit has room to spread. Like other Aponogeton bulb plants, A. natans may enter a natural dormancy where it dies back to the bulb; if this happens, leave the bulb in place or store it in damp sand for a few weeks until new shoots appear, then replant. Sources disagree mildly on adult height — Flowgrow cites ~11.5 cm blade length (just the blade, not stem+blade), Plants&Fins cites up to 76 cm and lists origin as Madagascar (likely confused with A. madagascariensis and discounted here), while Florida Aquatic, PetZone, and GlassAqua cluster around 20–51 cm including stem; the 20–50 cm range is used.
breedingNotes: Propagates primarily via the bulb (rhizome/tuberous corm) and from seed; mature plants periodically produce a long emergent flower spike that can self- or cross-pollinate to set seed in floating conditions. In aquaria, vegetative bulb division is the typical method — split offsets that develop on a healthy parent bulb. Seed propagation is uncommon in standard submerged setups but is documented in the wild and in dedicated emergent culture.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Manifest-supplied Tropica search URL returned only navigation/footer content for this species; Tropica does not currently appear to carry A. natans on a dedicated plant-details page (their Aponogeton catalogue covers crispus, ulvaceus, boivinianus, madagascariensis, and longiplumulosus, but not natans). Recorded as primary per manifest assignment, but the actual research data was gathered from the secondaries listed below.

additional.name: Buce Plant — aquarium plants catalogue
additional.notes: Manifest-supplied secondary; A. natans is not listed in Buce Plant's current aquarium-plants collection. Walked down the fallback chain to PetZone SD, GlassAqua, Florida Aquatic Nurseries, Plants&Fins, and Flowgrow per the playbook's secondary-failure procedure.

additional.name: PetZone SD — Aponogeton Natans
additional.notes: Fallback secondary. Confirmed origin (India, Southeast Asia), temperature 20–28 °C (68–82 °F), pH 6.0–7.5, low-to-moderate lighting (moderate preferred), CO2 optional, moderate growth, midground-to-background placement, propagation via bulb/seed, easy care level.

additional.name: Glass Aqua — Aponogeton Natans
additional.notes: Fallback secondary. Confirmed origin India and Sri Lanka, height 8–20 in (~20–51 cm), low-to-medium lighting, CO2 optional, moderate growth, background placement, easy care. Also confirmed the species produces floating leaves at maturity and that aquarium specimens develop slightly ruffled submerged leaves.

additional.name: Florida Aquatic Nurseries — Aponogeton natans
additional.notes: Fallback secondary. Confirmed India/Sri Lanka origin, prefers soft water, medium lighting, medium growth rate, mid-to-background focal placement, dormancy behaviour (dies back to bulb, can be stored in moist sand until regrowth).

additional.name: Flowgrow — Aponogeton natans
additional.notes: Fallback secondary used to fill hardness (2–14 °dKH) and broader parameter tolerances (temperature 20–31 °C, pH 5–8, CO2 0–40 mg/L). Confirmed family Aponogetonaceae, habitat 'temporary stagnant waters in India and Sri Lanka', seed propagation, and floating-leaf habit. Flowgrow's '11.5 cm' figure refers to leaf-blade length only, not full plant height; conflict with other sources discussed in careNotes.
